title: Tremors and agitation in a 44-year-old male
presentation: A 44-year-old male is brought in by police from a shelter. He is tremulous, diaphoretic, and agitated. His shelter roommate says he drinks heavily and was his last seen drinking 36 hours ago. Vitals: BP 162/98, HR 136, RR 20, Temp 38.1C, SpO2 97%. Exam: coarse tremor of hands, diaphoresis, mild confusion, hyperreflexia.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Always give thiamine BEFORE glucose in alcoholics to prevent Wernicke encephalopathy. CIWA score guides benzodiazepine dosing. Delirium tremens = ICU admission.
